steps: Go to https://bitbucket.org and log in
Click your avatar in the bottom-left corner, then select 'Personal settings'
In the left sidebar, click 'App passwords' under 'Security'
Enter a label and select the required permissions (e.g., Repositories: Read, Pull requests: Read)
Click 'Create' and copy the password immediately — it is shown only once
Fill in the app name, callback URL, and permissions, then click 'Save' to get your Client ID and Secret

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: OAuth Consumer Key (Client ID)
credential_fields.description: The Key from your Bitbucket OAuth consumer, used as client_id in the OAuth 2.0 flow

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: OAuth Consumer Secret
credential_fields.description: The Secret from your Bitbucket OAuth consumer, used as client_secret in the OAuth 2.0 flow

notes: OAuth consumers are workspace-scoped — create them under the workspace that owns the repos
Navigate to workspace OAuth consumers at: https://bitbucket.org/<workspace>/workspace/settings/oauth-consumers
For personal/script use, App Passwords are simpler than full OAuth — use username + app password as Basic auth
Free tier available with unlimited public repos and up to 5 users for private repos
Access tokens obtained via OAuth expire and must be refreshed using the refresh token

test_endpoint.description: Get the authenticated user profile to verify your credentials
